steps.title: Keep the compound name unbroken
steps.content: The phrase after "used" needs a single object: the chemical compound aluminum oxide. The infinitive phrase "to make a glassy solid" should follow that object directly, with no comma interrupting the connection.

steps.title: Reject misplaced commas
steps.content: Choice A opens an appositive with a comma but does not close it. Choice B places a comma before the infinitive phrase. Choice C sets off the compound name as if it were nonessential and also puts a comma before "to make." The unpunctuated noun phrase works cleanly. The answer is D.
